title: Random Search: A Faster Alternative
concept: Randomly sampling hyperparameter combinations from ranges to find a high-quality setup much faster than evaluating every single grid cell.

exercises.prompt: Recap: If you use Grid Search with 4 options for max depth and 5 options for minimum samples, how many models do you train?
exercises.explain: Correct! Grid Search multiplies the options: 4 × 5 = 20 total models to train and compare.

cards.title: Throwing darts
cards.body: Imagine throwing darts at a dartboard. If you want to hit the center, you don't need to systematically touch every single square millimeter of the board one by one. By throwing just a few random darts across the board, you will likely get very close to the bullseye much faster!

cards.title: What is Random Search?
cards.body: Random Search is a faster tuning method. Instead of testing every single combination, the computer randomly selects and evaluates a set number of combinations from ranges of hyperparameters.

cards.title: The raffle drawing
cards.body: Think of Grid Search as buying every single raffle ticket in order to guarantee winning a prize (very expensive and slow). Random Search is like buying 10 tickets scattered randomly. You save a lot of money and time, while still having a great chance of winning a prize.

cards.title: Saving time in a massive space
cards.body:
Suppose you are tuning 4 knobs, leading to 1,000 possible combinations:
• Grid Search: Must train all 1,000 models.
• Random Search: You tell the computer to randomly try 20 combinations. You save 98% of the training time and still find a highly accurate setup.

cards.title: Random can be better
cards.body:
Myth: Random Search is always worse than Grid Search because it is random and might miss the absolute best combination.
Reality: Random Search is often better in practice! Because it doesn't get stuck on a pre-defined grid, it can test values you didn't think to write down, discovering hidden sweet spots.

left.text:
Tests every crossing point.
Very slow for many knobs.
Can skip the best values in between.

right.text:
Picks random points from ranges.
Much faster to run.
Explores values between grid lines.

exercises.prompt: How does Random Search select hyperparameter combinations to test?

options.text: It randomly samples combinations from ranges of values.
It systematically tests every single possible coordinate crossing.
It asks the user to manually guess each setting during training.
It only tests the number 1 for every hyperparameter.

exercises.explain: Correct! Random Search randomly samples combinations from ranges, making it much faster to run.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Random Search is usually faster than Grid Search when tuning a model with many hyperparameters.
exercises.explain: True! By only testing a budget of random combinations rather than all of them, Random Search saves huge amounts of time.

exercises.sentence: While Grid Search only tests the specific values on a list, Random Search can test ___ values in between them.
exercises.explain: Correct! Since Random Search samples from ranges, it can test values that weren't on a predefined grid line.

exercises.prompt: In which situation is Random Search the best choice?

options.text: You have 5 different hyperparameters with millions of possible combinations and limited time.
You only have 2 options to test in total.
You want to guarantee testing every single combination.

exercises.explain: When the combination space is massive and time is limited, Random Search finds a great setup quickly without training millions of models.

exercises.prompt: You have 2 hours to tune a complex model with 6 different hyperparameters. A full Grid Search would take 50 hours. How should you set up a Random Search to get a good model in time?

options.text: Set the computer to test a random selection of combinations for 2 hours, then take the best one.
Run the Grid Search anyway and cancel it after 2 hours, keeping whatever it was doing.
Manually guess and test 3 combinations yourself.
Disable all hyperparameters so the model does not train.

exercises.explain: Perfect! Random Search is great for time budgets. You can tell it to randomly sample combinations for exactly 2 hours, and it will likely find a highly accurate setup that is very close to the optimal one. Next, let's learn how we evaluate these settings!
